name: Echo Global Logistics TMS API
what_it_does: Echo Global Logistics REST API for technology-enabled freight brokerage and transportation management platform. Enables AI agents to manage freight rating and carrier quote retrieval for shipment cost optimization, handle shipment booking and tender automation across truckload, LTL, and intermodal, access real-time shipment tracking and carrier milestone updates for freight visibility, retrieve carrier performance and reliability scoring data, manage freight invoice audit and payment processing automation, handle multi-mode rate comparison and carrier selection optimization, access load board and spot market rate data for dynamic pricing, retrieve freight analytics and shipping cost trend data, manage shipment documentation including BOL and POD retrieval, and integrate freight data with ERP, WMS, and order management systems.
use_cases: Comparing freight rates from shipper automation agents
Booking truckload and LTL shipments from order fulfillment agents
Tracking freight milestone events from supply chain visibility agents
Auditing freight invoices from finance automation agents
not_for: International freight forwarding without domestic US freight focus
Parcel and last-mile delivery without freight brokerage and TMS needs
Enterprise TMS without freight brokerage marketplace integration
best_when: A shipper or logistics team using Echo Global wants AI agents to automate freight rating, carrier selection, shipment booking, tracking, and freight invoice audit across truckload and LTL modes.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Automated freight booking commits to carrier rates and creates billing obligations — implement approval workflows for automated spot market bookings. Rate data has validity windows; stale rates cause booking failures or billing disputes. Automated carrier selection without service level validation can route time-sensitive freight via slow carriers.

auth.notes: Echo Global uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-account credentials. API documentation at echo.com/technology. Webhooks for shipment status events. REST API with JSON. Chicago, Illinois HQ. Public company (ECHO NASDAQ). EchoShip shipper portal alongside API. 35,000+ carrier network.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — freight transaction-based pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-shipment transaction fees; API access included with freight brokerage relationship
pricing.notes: Chicago, Illinois. Founded 2005. Public (ECHO NASDAQ). $4B+ annual revenue. Technology-enabled freight brokerage. 35,000+ carrier network. Strong in mid-market shippers and 3PLs. EchoShip TMS platform. Competes with Coyote Logistics (UPS) and CH Robinson for freight brokerage market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per account

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Automated freight booking commits carrier rates with billing obligations; implement approval workflow for spot market bookings
Rate validity windows — freight rates have short validity windows (minutes to hours for spot); check rate expiration before booking; expired rates cause booking failures
Carrier availability variability — carrier capacity is dynamic; automated carrier selection based on rating data may encounter availability issues at booking time
BOL generation timing — Bill of Lading generation has timing dependencies on shipper and carrier data; validate data completeness before automated BOL requests
Invoice audit complexity — freight invoice audit requires matching against quoted rates, accessorials, and fuel surcharges; automated audit without exception rules misses billing errors
API access requires freight relationship — Echo API access is tied to active freight brokerage relationship; no standalone API access without shipping volume
agent_readiness.security_notes: Freight brokerage and TMS. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Freight rate, carrier, and shipment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Freight brokerage TMS. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Freight rate and carrier data.
